automotive-ev-battery.title: AI method could extend EV battery lifespan by 23%
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Researchers at Chalmers University of Technology have developed an artificial intelligence method that optimizes fast charging based on battery condition, extending EV battery lifespan by nearly 23 percent without increasing charging time. This advancement addresses a critical pain point in EV adoption by improving long-term battery durability and vehicle economics.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Mahindra to expand passenger EV line-up, ramp up production capacity
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Indian automaker Mahindra announced plans to launch six new passenger electric vehicles over the next five years while increasing production capacity by 50 percent. The expansion reflects Mahindra's commitment to capturing growing EV market demand in the Asia-Pacific region.

automotive-ev-battery.title: MAN Truck & Bus wins Lithuanian city bus contract
automotive-ev-battery.summary: MAN Truck & Bus secured an order for 112 fully electric Lion's City E buses for Vilnius, Lithuania, comprising 84 solo buses and 28 articulated buses. This represents the largest electric bus procurement in Lithuanian history and demonstrates significant momentum in European public transport electrification.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Geely, VW and BYD retain lead in EV innovation ranking
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Geely, the Volkswagen Group, and BYD maintained their positions as global leaders in automotive EV innovation according to the Electromobility Report 2026 by CAM research. The ranking reflects these companies' sustained competitive advantage in electric mobility technology and market strategy.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Driivz to support rollout of Duracell E-Charge network in the UK
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Driivz partnered with Duracell E-Charge to provide software infrastructure for an ultra-fast EV charging network rollout across the United Kingdom. The platform will manage charging operations, billing, roaming capabilities, and remote diagnostics for the expanding network.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Toyota and Hyroad to scale H2 trucking in California
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Toyota Motor North America and Hyroad Energy announced a joint initiative to scale hydrogen-powered heavy-duty transportation in Southern California, with plans to deploy 40 new hydrogen fuel cell Class 8 trucks. The collaboration integrates fueling infrastructure, vehicles, and logistics support to advance zero-emission freight transportation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Jeep Avenger Electric qualifies for UK EV grant
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The fully electric Jeep Avenger now qualifies for the UK government's Electric Car Grant, reducing prices by £1,500 and making the compact electric SUV available from £28,499. The qualification improves affordability for consumers and expands market appeal for the Avenger Electric in the UK.

automotive-ev-battery.title: 34 Northvolt-sized battery factories could be lost if EU scales back EV targets
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A Transport & Environment report warns that weakening EU climate regulations for automobiles could result in the loss of investment equivalent to 34 Northvolt-sized battery manufacturing facilities. The analysis underscores the significant industrial opportunity cost of retreating from aggressive EV adoption targets in Europe.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Iveco Bus to build electric buses for Limoges metropolitan area
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Iveco Bus received orders to deliver electric buses and associated charging infrastructure for the Limoges metropolitan area in France, covering both articulated and solo bus configurations. The orders reflect continued European momentum toward transit electrification.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Opel Corsa GSE to become fastest production Opel
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Opel unveiled the Corsa GSE, an all-electric performance variant of its compact car that accelerates from zero to 100 kilometers per hour in 5.5 seconds, making it the fastest production Opel model to date. The hot hatch builds on technical specifications from the Mokka GSE and is scheduled for launch later this year.

tech-ai.title: Medicare's new payment model is built for AI, and most of the tech world has no idea
tech-ai.summary: Medicare has established the first governmental payment mechanism for AI agents that provide continuous patient monitoring and care coordination between visits. The ACCESS model represents a significant regulatory milestone that could reshape how healthcare AI systems are compensated and deployed across the medical industry.

tech-ai.title: Report: Google and SpaceX in talks to put data centers into orbit
tech-ai.summary: Google and SpaceX are exploring a partnership to build orbital data centers as a potential future infrastructure for AI compute, despite current costs substantially exceeding ground-based alternatives. The initiative reflects growing interest in space-based solutions to meet the massive computational demands of artificial intelligence systems.

tech-ai.title: Musk mulled handing OpenAI to his children, Altman testifies
tech-ai.summary: During testimony in Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, CEO Sam Altman revealed that Musk considered transferring control of the AI company to his family members. Altman expressed concern that such centralized control would contradict OpenAI's founding mission to prevent advanced AI from being held by a single person.

tech-ai.title: Sam Altman says Elon Musk's mind games were damaging OpenAI
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman testified that Elon Musk's management practices, including forcing researcher rankings and aggressive cost-cutting, inflicted significant cultural damage on the startup. Altman argued that Musk's leadership style, while effective at Tesla, proved incompatible with OpenAI's research-focused mission.

tech-ai.title: The AI legal services industry is heating up — Anthropic is getting in on the action
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic has launched new AI tools designed to automate clerical functions in law firms, including document review, case research, deposition preparation, and contract drafting. The move signals growing competition in the emerging AI legal services sector as major AI companies expand into professional services.

tech-ai.title: Everything Google announced at its Android Show, from Googlebooks to vibe-coded widgets
tech-ai.summary: Google unveiled a comprehensive suite of AI-first announcements including new Googlebooks laptops, enhanced Gemini features, AI-generated widgets, and improved integration across Android, Chrome, and other services. The announcements position Google's AI assistant as a central component of its ecosystem strategy ahead of its I/O conference.

tech-ai.title: Kevin Hartz's A* just closed its third fund with $450M
tech-ai.summary: Venture firm A* has closed its third fund at $450 million, maintaining its generalist approach across AI applications, fintech, healthcare, and security sectors. The fund plans to back at least 30 startups with average check sizes between $3 million and $5 million.

tech-ai.title: Google adds Gemini-powered dictation to Gboard, which could be bad news for dictation startups
tech-ai.summary: Google has integrated Gemini-powered transcription capabilities into Gboard, with initial rollout on Samsung Galaxy and Google Pixel phones. The move represents Google's continued expansion of AI features across its product lineup and poses potential competitive pressure on specialized dictation software vendors.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic warns investors against secondary platforms offering access to its shares
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic has cautioned investors that secondary markets offering access to its shares are unauthorized, warning that any stock transactions through these platforms will not be recognized on the company's books. The statement reflects heightened scrutiny around private share trading and valuation clarity for major AI companies.

tech-ai.title: Potholes cost cities millions: This company is using AI and trucks to fix them
tech-ai.summary: Fleet management company Samsara has developed an AI model capable of detecting various pothole types and predicting deterioration rates to improve infrastructure maintenance. The technology enables cities to optimize repair scheduling and reduce costs associated with road damage.

science-space.title: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket finally has a debut launch date. Here's when it will fly
science-space.summary: SpaceX has officially announced a May 19 launch date for the debut of Starship Version 3, an advanced megarocket designed to support humanity's missions to the moon and Mars. This milestone represents a significant step forward in commercial space exploration and deep space capability.

science-space.title: 'Cannibal stars,' AI and the Rubin Observatory could shed light on the mystery of dark energy. Here's how
science-space.summary: Scientists are leveraging artificial intelligence and data from the Rubin Observatory to reexamine Type 1a supernovae as cosmological 'standard candles' in an effort to uncover the nature of dark energy. This research approach may reveal previously unknown factors contributing to the universe's accelerating expansion.

science-space.title: Uranus and Neptune could be full of rocks, new study suggests
science-space.summary: A new study challenges the traditional classification of Uranus and Neptune as 'ice giants,' suggesting instead that these distant planets may contain substantial rocky material throughout their interiors. The research prompts scientists to reconsider the fundamental composition and formation history of these outer planets.

science-space.title: Hello Universe: NASA's Next-Gen Space Processor Undergoes Testing
science-space.summary: NASA's High Performance Spaceflight Computing project is developing advanced processors capable of withstanding the harsh space environment while dramatically increasing spacecraft computing power. These upgraded chips are essential for enabling autonomous spacecraft operations and accelerating the pace of scientific discovery in future missions.

science-space.title: Star Catcher just raised $65 million to build the world's first power grid in space — with lasers
science-space.summary: Star Catcher Industries has secured $65 million in funding to develop a constellation of power-beaming satellites using laser technology to transmit energy in space. The company aims to establish the world's first orbital power grid to support the growing off-Earth economy.

science-space.title: NASA's Psyche asteroid probe will fly within 3,000 miles of Mars on May 15: Here's what to expect
science-space.summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft, bound for the metal-rich asteroid of the same name, will execute a close Mars flyby on May 15 to utilize the planet's gravity for a trajectory boost. This gravitational assist maneuver is a crucial milestone in the probe's journey to study one of the solar system's most intriguing objects.

science-space.title: Thousands of deaths per year caused by invisible wildfire pollutant, satellite data shows
science-space.summary: Satellite data spanning two decades reveals that wildfire-derived ozone is responsible for approximately 2,045 excess deaths annually across the United States. This research highlights an often-overlooked public health consequence of wildfires and the utility of space-based monitoring for environmental health assessment.

science-space.title: Watch SpaceX launch 6,500 pounds of cargo to the International Space Station on May 13
science-space.summary: SpaceX will launch its Dragon cargo capsule toward the International Space Station on May 13, delivering approximately 6,500 pounds of supplies and equipment to the orbiting laboratory. The mission represents continued routine cargo resupply operations supporting the ISS crew.

science-space.title: NASA's Perseverance Rover Snaps Selfie in Mars' Western Frontier
science-space.summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a detailed self-portrait at a location called 'Lac de Charmes' during its exploration of previously unvisited terrain west of Jezero Crater. The composite image, assembled from 61 individual photographs, documents the rover's continued scientific investigation of Mars' geological features.

science-space.title: Cowboy Space raises $275 million to launch AI data centers on brand-new rocket
science-space.summary: Cowboy Space Corp. has raised $275 million in funding to develop homegrown rocket technology and operate artificial intelligence data centers in orbit. The company's dual focus on launch capability and orbital infrastructure positions it as a new player in the commercial space economy.

finance-markets.title: Kevin Warsh to face resurgent inflation and an impatient Trump as Fed chair
finance-markets.summary: Kevin Warsh, a 56-year-old financier, is expected to be confirmed by the US Senate this week as the next Federal Reserve chairman, replacing Jay Powell. Warsh will inherit significant challenges including persistent inflation pressures and demands from President Trump for monetary policy changes.

finance-markets.title: US inflation jumps to 3.8% as Trump's Iran war sends petrol prices soaring
finance-markets.summary: US inflation rose to 3.8% in April, marking the highest level in three years, driven significantly by surging petrol prices resulting from escalating conflict with Iran. The inflation surge poses a critical challenge for monetary policy and consumer purchasing power in the world's largest economy.

finance-markets.title: UK borrowing costs surge as Starmer leadership crisis rattles bond markets
finance-markets.summary: The UK's thirty-year gilt yield has reached its highest level this century as a cabinet crisis undermines confidence in Prime Minister Starmer's leadership. The market turbulence reflects investor concerns over political stability and potential fiscal policy uncertainty in the second-largest European economy.

finance-markets.title: Blue Owl retail fundraising evaporates amid private credit concerns
finance-markets.summary: Blue Owl's retail fundraising has collapsed to a fraction of prior year levels as investor concerns mount regarding potential defaults in private credit markets. The sharp decline signals deteriorating confidence in the alternative credit sector and heightened risk perception among retail investors.

finance-markets.title: Trump's plan to discuss Taiwan arms sales with Xi rattles Asian allies
finance-markets.summary: President Trump's proposed negotiations with China on US weapons sales to Taiwan have alarmed regional allies, as such discussions would represent a significant departure from established US policy. The potential talks highlight growing uncertainty in US-China relations and Asia-Pacific geopolitical dynamics with direct implications for defense and trade.

finance-markets.title: Stocks Pare Losses, Bonds Fall After Inflation: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Asian equities recovered from initial losses as dip buyers stepped in following weakness in chipmakers, while bonds declined sharply after stronger-than-expected US inflation data. The market reaction reflects growing speculation that the Federal Reserve may need to raise interest rates in the coming year, shifting expectations for monetary policy.

finance-markets.title: China's big tech groups miss out on AI stock market frenzy
finance-markets.summary: Major Chinese technology companies including Tencent and Alibaba have been left behind as global investors focus capital allocation on pure-play AI stocks. The divergence underscores investor preference for dedicated artificial intelligence businesses and potential concerns about Chinese tech giants' AI capabilities and market positioning.

finance-markets.title: India's Economic Strength Defies Foreign Outflow Fears, S&P Says
finance-markets.summary: According to S&P Global Ratings, India's underlying economic strength is weathering global financial pressures better than headline indicators suggest, with concerns over foreign capital outflows appearing overstated. The assessment indicates India's relative resilience amid volatile global financial conditions and emerging market pressures.

finance-markets.title: Indonesia Vows 'Smart Interventions' as Rupiah Falls
finance-markets.summary: Indonesia's central bank has pledged strategic foreign-exchange market interventions as the rupiah has fallen to a record low. The currency weakness reflects broader emerging market pressures and signals potential capital outflows amid regional financial volatility.

finance-markets.title: It's not just Iran and oil raising inflation. Prices also are reaccelerating in these other areas
finance-markets.summary: Beyond energy prices driven by Iran tensions, inflation is accelerating across multiple consumer categories including shelter and food. The broadening price pressures across diverse sectors signal systemic inflationary challenges extending beyond commodity-specific shocks.

world-news.title: Trump set to meet with Xi in Beijing as war and inflation weigh on his presidency
world-news.summary: President Donald Trump arrives in Beijing for a state visit with Chinese President Xi Jinping amid global tensions over ongoing conflicts and trade disputes. The summit represents a critical diplomatic engagement between the two superpowers at a time of escalating geopolitical instability.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Trump travels to China as conflict with Tehran looms large
world-news.summary: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have resulted in 2,883 deaths and 8,787 injuries since March 2, according to Lebanon's Health Ministry, as regional conflict escalates. The ongoing Iran-Israel tensions form a critical backdrop to Trump's diplomatic visit to China.

world-news.title: Lebanon says two paramedics among 13 killed in Israeli strikes
world-news.summary: Lebanon's health ministry reported that two paramedics were killed during rescue operations in Nabatieh following Israeli military strikes. The incident highlights the ongoing casualties among civilian and medical personnel in the regional conflict.

world-news.title: US inflation jumps to 3.8% as energy costs surge from Iran war
world-news.summary: United States inflation rose to 3.8%, its highest level since May 2023, driven by increased energy costs stemming from regional conflict with Iran. The spike directly impacts consumer purchasing power and poses challenges to the broader global economy.

world-news.title: US court pauses decision blocking Trump's 10 percent global tariff
world-news.summary: A federal court has temporarily paused a decision that would have blocked Trump's 10 percent global tariff policy, as a coalition of 24 states challenges its legality under the 1974 Trade Act. The ruling keeps the tariffs in effect and has significant implications for global trade relations.

world-news.title: Zelensky's ex-chief of staff in court as Ukraine corruption probe escalates
world-news.summary: Andriy Yermak, former chief of staff to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, faces investigation by Ukraine's anti-corruption agencies on money-laundering charges. The high-profile case marks an escalation in Ukraine's domestic corruption efforts during its ongoing conflict.

world-news.title: Uganda's president sworn in for record seventh term
world-news.summary: Uganda's President Yoweri Museveni, aged 81, has been sworn in for his seventh term following disputed January elections that extend his 40-year rule. The outcome raises concerns about democratic governance and authoritarianism in East Africa.

world-news.title: South Africa's top court bars repeat asylum applications
world-news.summary: South Africa's highest court has ruled to prevent repeat asylum applications, a decision the home affairs minister characterizes as a "victory" against alleged system abuse. The ruling has significant implications for refugee policy and human rights in southern Africa.

world-news.title: No sign of larger hantavirus outbreak, says UN health agency
world-news.summary: The World Health Organization reports no evidence of a widespread hantavirus outbreak but warns that the situation remains fluid with potential for additional confirmed cases. The assessment reflects ongoing global health surveillance in response to infectious disease threats.

world-news.title: Peru presidential candidate Roberto Sanchez charged with financial crimes
world-news.summary: Peruvian prosecutors have charged leftist presidential candidate Roberto Sanchez with financial crimes and sought a sentence of five years and four months for false financial disclosures. The charges carry implications for Peru's upcoming electoral process and democratic institutions.

startups-vc.title: Kevin Hartz's A* just closed its third fund with $450M
startups-vc.summary: A* Capital has closed its third venture fund at $450 million, taking a generalist approach across AI applications, fintech, healthcare, and security sectors. The firm plans to deploy capital through checks averaging between $3 million and $5 million, targeting investments in at least 30 startups.

startups-vc.title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
startups-vc.summary: Nvidia has committed $40 billion to equity investments in AI companies during 2026, demonstrating the technology giant's continued dominance in shaping the AI startup ecosystem. The substantial commitment underscores Nvidia's strategic expansion beyond its core hardware business into venture capital financing.

startups-vc.title: Frontier Labs And Robotics Companies Again Top List Of New Unicorns In April
startups-vc.summary: Twenty-eight companies achieved unicorn status in April 2026, with frontier AI labs and robotics startups leading the cohort for the second consecutive month. The data reflects investor appetite for deep technology companies building foundational models and autonomous systems.

startups-vc.title: European AI Funding Is Growing. Will That Boost The Region's Startup Scene?
startups-vc.summary: European venture capital is increasingly focused on AI, with a growing percentage of 2026 funding directed toward both frontier model companies and diverse AI-centric startups across the region. The trend suggests a potential rebalancing of the global AI investment landscape away from Silicon Valley concentration.

startups-vc.title: Meesho Goes Public
startups-vc.summary: Indian e-commerce platform Meesho, which graduated from Y Combinator's S16 cohort, has completed its initial public offering after a decade of building a marketplace for small merchants. The IPO represents a major exit for one of India's most significant startup ecosystem success stories.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to EquipmentShare on Going Public
startups-vc.summary: EquipmentShare, a Y Combinator W15 graduate, has gone public after scaling from a Missouri-based operation to 373 locations nationwide in the construction equipment space. The company's IPO marks a significant milestone for the startup ecosystem and demonstrates success in building enterprise software for a traditionally fragmented industry.

startups-vc.title: Fintech startup Parker files for bankruptcy
startups-vc.summary: Parker, a well-funded corporate credit card and banking services fintech startup, has filed for bankruptcy and ceased operations. The shutdown highlights ongoing challenges in the competitive fintech sector despite significant venture capital backing.

startups-vc.title: AI Is Rewriting What Investors Should Look For In Early Startup Teams
startups-vc.summary: As AI tools democratize product development capabilities, technical expertise alone no longer serves as a meaningful competitive differentiator for early-stage startups, forcing investors to reassess what actually constitutes defensible value. The shift raises fundamental questions about team evaluation criteria in an era when product building has become commoditized.

startups-vc.title: Who's Winning Enterprise AI Now: Claude Up 128%, Gemini Up 48%, OpenAI Down 8%, Grok Still A Rounding Error
startups-vc.summary: Recent Enterprise Technology Research data reveals significant shifts in enterprise AI model adoption over the past twelve months, with Anthropic's Claude gaining 128 percent year-over-year adoption while OpenAI's market share declined 8 percent. The findings suggest a competitive reshaping in the enterprise AI landscape as multiple providers vie for adoption among large organizations.

startups-vc.title: OpenAI Has Already Created 300+ Decamillionaires. More Than a Decade of B2B IPOs Combined. Before Going Public.
startups-vc.summary: OpenAI's October 2025 tender offer enabled approximately 600 current and former employees to sell shares, with roughly 75 individuals receiving the $30 million maximum allocation and 525 others averaging approximately $8.3 million each. The single wealth event demonstrates the extraordinary valuation multiples and equity concentration within high-performing AI startups prior to public markets.

crypto-web3.title: JPMorgan files to launch new tokenized fund as Wall Street tokenization race heats up
crypto-web3.summary: JPMorgan is expanding its tokenized money market fund offerings on blockchain networks, following BlackRock's recent entry into the space. The move underscores accelerating institutional adoption of tokenized financial products among major Wall Street players.

crypto-web3.title: The Ethereum Foundation unveils new 'Clear Signing' standard to stop users from approving malicious crypto transactions
crypto-web3.summary: The Ethereum Foundation has introduced a new Clear Signing standard designed to make cryptocurrency transaction approvals more transparent and easier for users to understand. The initiative addresses billions in losses from phishing attacks and unauthorized wallet drains by improving transaction readability.

crypto-web3.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh to Fed board ahead of expected Chair vote
crypto-web3.summary: The Senate has confirmed crypto-linked investor Kevin Warsh to the Federal Reserve board, positioning him as a potential successor to Chair Jerome Powell. Warsh's appointment carries significance for digital asset policy as debates over inflation and cryptocurrency regulation intensify.

crypto-web3.title: Privacy emerges as crypto's next 'killer app,' with Arc, Canton and Tempo topping $1 billion in funding
crypto-web3.summary: Three institution-focused privacy blockchains have surpassed one billion dollars in aggregate funding, signaling privacy as a critical priority in enterprise cryptocurrency infrastructure. The fundraising momentum reflects how regulation, institutional demand, and competitive pressures are reshaping the blockchain ecosystem.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. CFTC in talks with every major pro sports league on policing prediction markets
crypto-web3.summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is engaging with all major professional sports leagues to regulate prediction markets and enforce derivatives law. The agency has already initiated legal proceedings against multiple states to establish that sports contracts constitute regulated derivatives rather than simple wagers.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin's bull-bear cycle indicator turns green for first time since March 2023
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin's bull-bear cycle indicator has shifted to green for the first time in over two years, potentially signaling entry into an early bull market phase. While some analysts identify $90,000 as the next key resistance level, experts caution that this technical signal does not guarantee future price performance.

crypto-web3.title: JPMorgan launching second tokenized money market fund on Ethereum
crypto-web3.summary: JPMorgan is launching a second tokenized money market fund on the Ethereum blockchain, investing in U.S. Treasurys and overnight repurchase agreements. The expansion demonstrates the bank's commitment to blockchain-based financial products and tokenization infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: CFTC backs Kalshi in Ohio fight over prediction markets jurisdictional battle
crypto-web3.summary: The CFTC has expressed support for prediction market platform Kalshi in its legal dispute with Ohio over regulatory jurisdiction. The agency's involvement underscores ongoing legal battles regarding which authorities have the power to regulate cryptocurrency-based prediction markets.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase adds Solana-backed loans through Morpho, letting users borrow up to $100,000
crypto-web3.summary: Coinbase has integrated Morpho lending protocols on its Base layer network, enabling users to borrow up to $100,000 against Solana token holdings. The offering expands Coinbase's lending capabilities and provides users with additional options for utilizing their crypto assets.

crypto-web3.title: Benchmark joins others in reiterating Buy rating on Coinbase stock despite lackluster Q1 earnings
crypto-web3.summary: Benchmark equity analysts have maintained a Buy rating on Coinbase stock and raised their price target to $270 from $260, despite the exchange reporting underwhelming first-quarter earnings. The analyst support suggests continued confidence in the company's long-term growth prospects.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Why Marty Makary was the worst FDA commissioner in 25 years
health-biotech.summary: Marty Makary, who resigned under pressure from the FDA, brought to the job a fundamental lack of understanding of the nature of the role, STAT's Matthew Herper writes.

health-biotech.title: Males who discuss suicide seek help less often than females, report finds
health-biotech.summary: Out of 1.5 million messages received in 2025, the Crisis Text Line found that less then 20% of the texters identified as male only

health-biotech.title: STAT+: European Union inks a draft deal to boost local production of medicines and avoid shortages
health-biotech.summary: The E.U. reached a provisional deal to strengthen the supply of essential medicines and avoid shortages by ‌boosting domestic production and reducing reliance on imports.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Makary departs FDA amid turmoil as Diamantas, agency’s top food official, steps in
health-biotech.summary: Food and Drug Administration Commissioner Marty Makary is resigning from his role after a tumultuous tenure.

health-biotech.title: Supreme Court extends mifepristone deadline
health-biotech.summary: The deadliest drug in the country, the new name for PCOS, and more health news from Morning Rounds

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Hims takes a $33 million hit from GLP-1 ‘pivot’
health-biotech.summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: Hims earnings, Omada's PBM strategy, and the uphill battle faced by new sepsis algorithms.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Hims’ sales miss as telehealth competition grows
health-biotech.summary: Bristol's partnership with Hengrui Pharma, Viiv loses HIV treatment case, and more biotech news from The Readout

health-biotech.title: STAT+: STAT examines America’s deadliest drug
health-biotech.summary: STAT examines America's failure to address excessive alcohol use across decades.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about pharma lobbying, Bristol’s deal with a Chinese partner, and more
health-biotech.summary: The 17 pharmaceutical companies anchoring TrumpRx poured more than $130 million into federal lobbying in 2025 — a nearly 23% surge

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Capsida says it still doesn’t know what caused gene therapy death
health-biotech.summary: Some in the gene therapy field lament lost time while Capsida kept silent about the brain receptor it was targeting when things went wrong.

cybersecurity.title: RubyGems Suspends New Signups After Hundreds of Malicious Packages Are Uploaded
cybersecurity.summary: RubyGems, the primary package manager for Ruby programming language, has temporarily halted new account registrations following a significant malicious attack that resulted in the upload of hundreds of compromised packages. This incident represents a critical supply chain security threat affecting developers and organizations relying on the Ruby ecosystem.

cybersecurity.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Compromises TanStack, Mistral AI, Guardrails AI & More Packages
cybersecurity.summary: The threat actor TeamPCP has been linked to a fresh supply chain attack campaign compromising multiple npm and PyPI packages from major organizations including TanStack, Mistral AI, and OpenSearch. The infected packages contain obfuscated JavaScript designed to profile execution environments and exfiltrate sensitive data from development pipelines.

cybersecurity.title: Instructure Reaches Ransom Agreement with ShinyHunters to Stop 3.65TB Canvas Leak
cybersecurity.summary: Educational technology company Instructure, provider of the widely-used Canvas platform, has reached a settlement agreement with the ShinyHunters extortion group following a breach that exposed data from thousands of schools and universities. The incident highlights the targeting of critical educational infrastructure and the ongoing threat of data exfiltration attacks against large institutional providers.

cybersecurity.title: New Exim BDAT Vulnerability Exposes GnuTLS Builds to Potential Code Execution
cybersecurity.summary: Exim, a widely-deployed open-source Mail Transfer Agent, has released patches addressing a severe use-after-free vulnerability (CVE-2026-45185) that could enable memory corruption and remote code execution on vulnerable systems. This vulnerability affects critical email infrastructure across Unix-like systems globally.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle are addressing near-record volumes of security vulnerabilities, with artificial intelligence systems proving instrumental in identifying previously unknown bugs in human-written code. This accelerated patch cadence underscores the expanding attack surface and the dual-edged nature of AI-assisted security analysis.

cybersecurity.title: New TrickMo Variant Uses TON C2 and SOCKS5 to Create Android Network Pivots
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers have identified a new variant of the TrickMo Android banking trojan that leverages The Open Network (TON) blockchain for command-and-control communications to evade detection. Active between January and February 2026, the variant has targeted banking and cryptocurrency users across France, Italy, and Austria.

cybersecurity.title: 'CanisterWorm' Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
cybersecurity.summary: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group has deployed a self-propagating worm that targets systems configured with Iran's time zone or Farsi language settings, combining data exfiltration with destructive wiper functionality. The campaign exploits poorly secured cloud services to spread and represents an evolution in geopolitically-motivated cyber operations.

cybersecurity.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
cybersecurity.summary: A Brazilian cybersecurity firm specializing in DDoS mitigation has been implicated in enabling a botnet responsible for massive distributed denial-of-service attacks against competing network operators within Brazil. Company leadership attributed the malicious activity to a security breach, though allegations suggest potential competitive sabotage.

cybersecurity.title: Hugging Face Packages Weaponized With a Single File Tweak
cybersecurity.summary: A tokenizer library file embedded in popular Hugging Face AI models can be manipulated to hijack model outputs and exfiltrate sensitive data, representing a critical vulnerability in the machine learning supply chain. The trivial nature of the exploit demonstrates how single-file modifications in widely-distributed AI packages pose significant security risks.

cybersecurity.title: Cyber Espionage Group Targets Aviation Firms to Steal Map Data
cybersecurity.summary: A sophisticated cyber espionage campaign has quietly compromised aerospace and drone operators to systematically exfiltrate geographic information system files, terrain models, and GPS data. The coordinated intelligence gathering operation reveals adversary efforts to construct comprehensive geospatial intelligence pictures of operational areas.

energy-climate.title: Hyperscalers driving record clean energy deals: CEBA CEO
energy-climate.summary: U.S. corporate clean energy procurement surpassed 27 gigawatts in 2025, with hyperscalers accounting for approximately three-quarters of total capacity. This demonstrates unprecedented momentum in private sector renewable energy adoption driven by major technology companies.

energy-climate.title: ADB unveils $70bn plan for Asia's energy and digital infrastructure
energy-climate.summary: The Asian Development Bank has announced a $70 billion initiative to strengthen energy and digital infrastructure across Asia and the Pacific through 2035. This major investment addresses the region's growing energy demands and infrastructure modernization needs.

energy-climate.title: Con Edison to spend $29B shoring up NYC area grid as electrification rises
energy-climate.summary: Con Edison plans to invest $29 billion to upgrade the New York City area grid amid increasing demand from building and transportation electrification. The investment reflects utilities' critical role in enabling the transition to electrified energy systems.

energy-climate.title: Northland inks 30-year CPPA with TSMC for Hai Long wind project
energy-climate.summary: Northland Power has secured a 30-year corporate power purchase agreement with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company for its 1.02 GW Hai Long offshore wind project in Taiwan. The long-term agreement demonstrates major technology companies' commitment to securing stable renewable energy supplies.

energy-climate.title: Germany allocates 2.3 GW in latest ground-mounted PV tender, lowest bid comes in at €0.039/kWh
energy-climate.summary: Germany's latest photovoltaic tender round was heavily oversubscribed, allocating 2.3 gigawatts with winning bids as low as €0.039 per kilowatt-hour. The results demonstrate continued cost competitiveness of solar energy in European renewable energy markets.

energy-climate.title: How hail is rewriting the solar insurance rulebook
energy-climate.summary: Severe convective storms drove $60 billion in insured losses in 2025, reshaping the solar energy insurance market and forcing insurers to tighten capacity and raise premiums. Climate-related weather volatility is increasingly impacting the economic viability and risk profiles of solar projects.

energy-climate.title: Zambia's largest solar plant goes online
energy-climate.summary: Zambia's 136 megawatt Itimpi II solar plant has become the country's largest operational solar project, while an additional 250 megawatts is planned for expansion at the Nambala facility. The projects advance sub-Saharan Africa's renewable energy capacity and energy access goals.

energy-climate.title: All-perovskite tandem solar cell built with laser polishing achieves 29.80% efficiency
energy-climate.summary: Chinese researchers have developed an all-perovskite tandem solar cell achieving 29.80% efficiency through non-contact laser polishing that reduces surface defects and improves charge extraction. The breakthrough represents significant progress in next-generation photovoltaic technology with potential to increase solar energy conversion rates.

energy-climate.title: Jolywood starts mass production of low-silver TOPCon solar cells
energy-climate.summary: Chinese solar manufacturer Jolywood has begun mass production of a novel TOPCon cell design that replaces silver with aluminum paste while maintaining efficiency above 27%. The innovation addresses material costs and supply chain constraints critical to scaling solar manufacturing globally.

energy-climate.title: Hydrogen's next phase will favour execution over scale
energy-climate.summary: The hydrogen sector's market outlook indicates that renewable power access and durable policy frameworks will become the decisive factors determining which projects advance. The shift reflects recognition that successful hydrogen deployment requires careful implementation rather than rapid scaling without adequate infrastructure.

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong-listed biotech firm uses AI to produce nano-rockets to deliver life-saving drugs
asia-pacific.summary:
Shares of METiS TechBio surged 173 per cent on their Hong Kong debut on Wednesday as the AI-driven drug design start-up’s initial public offering drew strong demand from investors amid buoyant sentiment for AI-related stocks in the city.
Its stocks first traded at HK$28.68, significantly higher than its offering price of HK$10.50 each.
Co-founder and CEO of METiS TechBio, Lai Tsai-Ta, who obtained a PhD from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in the US, said artificial intelligence had...

asia-pacific.title: Singaporeans refute lack of hunger stereotype, warn of ‘self-fulfilling bias’ over jobs
asia-pacific.summary:
Azizul Kamal has spent a decade building his legal career in Singapore, but half of the time, he was hopping from one contract role to another in the industry.
“Every company seems to only want to hire contractors, because we’re cheaper for balance books. It’s very hard to find a full-time role,” the 38-year-old Singaporean said.
Azizul did not secure a third extension as a lawyer at his previous firm, after it found a permanent replacement based in Kuala Lumpur, he said. With bills to pay, he..

asia-pacific.title: US’ scientific self-harm will only help China
asia-pacific.summary:
After the Louisiana Purchase from France in 1803, which almost doubled the size of the United States’ territory, US president Thomas Jefferson commissioned the legendary Lewis and Clark expedition, which surveyed new routes from the Missouri river to the Pacific coast. It also carried out work in agriculture, ethnography (with indigenous peoples) and geography.
Throughout the 19th and early 20th centuries, that was the kind of science the US government was willing to pay for – practical,...

asia-pacific.title: Thailand to review visa rules after spate of tourist crimes trigger public anger
asia-pacific.summary:
Thailand is poised to overhaul its liberal visa rules as the government ramps up a crackdown on foreigners who illegally run businesses or commit transnational crimes.
Possible measures include reducing the 60-day visa-free stay for tourists to 30 days and reviewing the criteria for categories, including investment, long-term stay, student and digital nomad visas, according to officials. Visitors from 93 countries currently are eligible for the 60-day waiver.
Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul..

asia-pacific.title: Privacy screens and police dogs: how Beijing is preparing for Trump’s visit
asia-pacific.summary:
Beijing intensified security and prepared key venues on Tuesday as the Chinese capital awaited the arrival of US President Donald Trump and his high-stakes talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping later this week.
Trump is expected to stay at the five-star Four Seasons Hotel in northeastern Beijing after arriving on Wednesday night. His delegation is expected to stay at the nearby Kempinski Hotel Beijing Yansha Centre.
While neither country has officially disclosed the delegation’s lodging, rooms

asia-pacific.title: How Australia’s mining giants are helping China to globalise the yuan
asia-pacific.summary:
Australia’s mining giants are aiding China’s push to internationalise its currency and reduce the dominance of the US dollar, as they gradually shift towards using the yuan for financing and settlements, analysts said.
China has been using its heft as the world’s dominant iron ore buyer to push global mining companies to adopt the yuan. And several firms are already making the switch, with China’s relatively low interest rates becoming an added incentive.
BHP, the world’s largest mining company,

asia-pacific.title: China actor who has stopped growing at 9 is known for playing child roles to build acting career
asia-pacific.summary:
An actor in China nicknamed the “ageless man” because of a childhood condition which halted his growth has silenced detractors with his nuanced performances.
According to Beiqing.com, Hou Xiang, 40, from Beijing, was born prematurely after his mother suffered from malnutrition during pregnancy.
His growth and voice development stopped around the age of nine, leaving him less than 1.6 metres tall.
Even in adulthood, strangers often mistake Hou for a boy, asking his age or where he studies.

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong teens dive deep to safeguard global shipping lanes
asia-pacific.summary:
[The content of this article has been produced by our advertising partner.]
A group of Grade 8 students from Canadian International School of Hong Kong (CDNIS) has made waves on the global innovation stage, returning from the Conrad Challenge Global Innovation Summit in Houston with multiple top honours. The team captured the coveted Innovation Summit Power Pitch Award and the Equinor Searching for Better Award – achievements that also earned them a sponsored educational trip to Boston,...

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong to launch public consultation on 5-year blueprint ‘by early June’
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong is expected to launch a public consultation in early June for its inaugural five-year plan to align with the national blueprint, with Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu set to present a preliminary framework and gather feedback from various industries through face-to-face sessions, according to sources.
The South China Morning Post learned that the government was also planning a large-scale publicity campaign to promote the consultation exercise, including television advertisements,...

asia-pacific.title: Southeast Asia warned of ‘Godzilla El Nino’ whiplash threatening drought, floods, haze
asia-pacific.summary:
Southeast Asia must brace itself for a punishing spell of climate whiplash, with an expected El Nino threatening drought-like conditions, flash floods, crop losses and haze across the region, experts have warned.
The threat is also landing at a fragile moment for emerging economies, analysts add, as geopolitical tensions, higher energy costs and pressure on remittances leave governments and households with less room to absorb climate shocks.
Sea surface temperatures in the equatorial Pacific...
